description: Evaluates the testing strategy skill across different system types, test shapes, and advanced techniques. Each eval targets a specific testing architecture decision or capability.

evals.name: backend-api-pyramid-shape
evals.prompt: Design a test strategy for a business-logic-heavy Java Spring Boot API with 40 REST endpoints and PostgreSQL database.
evals.expected_output: Full 6-section strategy: pyramid shape selected for backend API, JUnit 5 + Testcontainers automation, contract testing if consumers exist, performance testing, test data management, and quality metrics.
expectations: Pyramid shape selected with 70:20:10 ratio (unit-heavy for business logic)
JUnit 5 recommended for unit tests, Spring Boot Test + Testcontainers for integration
Coverage target: 80% line coverage for business logic, 60% overall
Test categorization: fast (<1s unit), medium (<10s integration), slow (<60s E2E)
CI trigger mapping: unit on push, integration on PR, E2E on merge to main
Test data strategy uses Testcontainers for integration tests
Mutation testing recommended for critical business logic modules

evals.name: frontend-spa-trophy-shape
evals.prompt: Build test automation for a React TypeScript SPA with complex component interactions and a design system.
evals.expected_output: Trophy shape selected for frontend SPA: integration-heavy with Testing Library, Playwright for E2E, Chromatic for visual regression.
expectations: Trophy shape selected with 20:50:20 ratio + 10% static analysis
Testing Library recommended for integration tests (best ROI for component interactions)
Vitest or Jest for unit tests
Playwright for E2E on critical user journeys
Chromatic or Percy for visual regression testing of design system
Shift-left strategy includes pre-commit linting and type checking
Local developer experience target: tests run in <30s for changed module

evals.name: microservices-contract-testing
evals.prompt: Implement contract testing for our order management system with 8 microservices and 15 consumer relationships.
evals.expected_output: Deep S3 (Contract Testing): consumer-driven contracts with Pact, Pact Broker workflow, can-i-deploy gates, backward compatibility detection.
expectations: Consumer-driven contracts recommended for internal microservices with multiple consumers
Pact workflow described: consumer writes test -> generates contract -> provider verifies
Pact Broker for contract storage and version management
can-i-deploy check before production promotion
Backward compatibility breaking change detection in CI
Contract testing reduces O(N*M) E2E to O(N+M) per skill documentation
Event contract testing addressed if async messaging present

evals.name: legacy-no-tests-edge-case
evals.prompt: Define quality gates for a 10-year-old PHP monolith with zero test coverage that we need to stabilize before modernization.
evals.expected_output: Legacy edge case handled: starts with characterization tests, adds integration around critical paths, unit tests for new code only. Does not attempt retroactive 80% coverage.
expectations: Starts with characterization tests to capture current behavior
Adds integration tests around critical business paths first
Unit tests required for new code only (not retroactive coverage)
Does NOT attempt 80% coverage retroactively on legacy code
Ice cream cone shape acknowledged as starting point, with plan to invert
Quality gates are pragmatic: passing characterization tests, no regression on critical paths
Flaky test management policy defined from the start

evals.name: chaos-engineering-maturity
evals.prompt: Manage test data and introduce chaos engineering for our Kubernetes-deployed payment processing system.
evals.expected_output: Deep S4 (Chaos) + S5 (Test Data): chaos maturity model starting at Level 1, fault injection patterns, blast radius control. Test data with PII anonymization for payment data.
expectations: Chaos maturity model starts at Level 1 (manual fault injection, staging only)
Fault injection patterns include pod termination, network latency, dependency unavailability
Blast radius control: start small (single pod), expand to zone, then region
Automatic rollback required if safety thresholds exceeded
Test data strategy includes PII anonymization for payment/financial data
Compliance addressed: no real customer payment data in non-production environments
Synthetic data generation for realistic payment scenarios
